record.name: D+Q bone RCT needs primary-null and subgroup-signal separation
record.summary: The postmenopausal women bone trial has a null primary 20-week CTx result for D+Q versus control, short-term P1NP secondary signals at 2 and 4 weeks, no 20-week P1NP difference, and exploratory subgroup signals tied to baseline senescent-cell burden.
record.statement: The phase 2 randomized bone trial should be represented as mixed human evidence: D+Q did not reduce the primary 20-week bone resorption marker CTx in the overall group, but secondary P1NP measures favored D+Q at 2 and 4 weeks and exploratory subgroup findings require separate labeling.
record.time_horizon: Phase 2 trial follow-up period for bone metabolism outcomes
caveats: Primary endpoint, exploratory subgroup, and biomarker outcomes must be extracted separately before any synthesis claim.
Bone metabolism evidence should not be generalized to global aging outcomes.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract key results for PMID 38956196
provenance.note: Abstract reports primary CTx, secondary P1NP, serious adverse event, and exploratory high-senescent-cell-burden findings.
ClinicalTrials.gov posted results provide group medians, ranges, p-values, and adverse-event aggregate counts.

record.name: D+Q 20-week adverse-event aggregate result

group_values.group_label: D+Q participants with other adverse events
Untreated-control participants with other adverse events
D+Q participants with serious adverse events
Untreated-control participants with serious adverse events

effect.raw_text: ClinicalTrials.gov adverseEventsModule reports 23/30 D+Q participants and 5/30 untreated-control participants with other adverse events; serious adverse events and deaths were 0/30 in both groups.

provenance.note: Posted aggregate adverse-event group counts.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract serious adverse event sentence
provenance.note: Abstract reports no serious adverse events were observed.

record.statement: D+Q had no posted serious adverse events or deaths in the registry aggregate counts, but other adverse events were more common in the D+Q arm than untreated control.
caveats: This aggregate record does not enumerate every adverse-event preferred term.
Safety interpretation should include event-specific extraction before a formal safety synthesis.

record.name: D+Q 20-week BMD descriptive result

effect.raw_text: ClinicalTrials.gov posts BMD group medians by DXA site but no pairwise D+Q versus control p-value in this outcome module.

analysis.comparison: D+Q versus untreated control by DXA site
analysis.effect_note: No pairwise analysis was posted in the ClinicalTrials.gov BMD outcome module.

provenance.note: Posted BMD group values by lumbar spine, femoral neck, and radius classes.

record.statement: The posted registry BMD module provides D+Q and control group values by site, but no pairwise analysis is posted for the overall BMD outcome.
caveats: BMD site-specific values should not be pooled without separate site-level outcomes.
PubMed abstract reports an exploratory radius BMD subgroup signal in high-senescent-cell-burden participants, which is not the same as this overall registry-posted BMD summary.

record.name: D+Q 20-week CTx primary outcome null result

effect.raw_text: ClinicalTrials.gov posted median percent change values for CTx at 20 weeks; D+Q versus untreated control superiority analysis p=0.611 by Wilcoxon (Mann-Whitney).

analysis.effect_note: No statistically significant D+Q effect on primary 20-week CTx outcome in the overall group.

provenance.note: Posted primary outcome group values and D+Q versus control p-value.
Abstract confirms primary CTx endpoint did not differ between groups.

record.statement: D+Q did not significantly change the primary 20-week CTx bone resorption marker versus untreated control.
caveats: Registry dispersion is full range; PubMed abstract reports interquartile ranges, so effect extraction should keep source-specific dispersion labels separate.
This result applies to the overall D+Q versus untreated-control comparison, not exploratory high-senescent-cell-burden subgroup analyses.

record.name: D+Q 20-week P1NP null secondary result

effect.raw_text: ClinicalTrials.gov posted median percent change values for P1NP at 20 weeks; D+Q versus untreated control superiority analysis p=0.149.

analysis.effect_note: No statistically significant D+Q effect on P1NP at 20 weeks.

provenance.note: Posted secondary outcome group values and D+Q versus control p-value.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract P1NP secondary endpoint sentence
provenance.note: Abstract reports P1NP was not different from control at 20 weeks.

record.statement: D+Q did not significantly change P1NP versus untreated control at 20 weeks.
caveats: This later secondary endpoint contrasts with nominal short-term P1NP signals at 2 and 4 weeks.

record.name: D+Q 2-week P1NP positive secondary result

effect.raw_text: Registry medians were D+Q 0.8 and control -15.2; PubMed abstract describes a +16% increase relative to control at 2 weeks.

analysis.effect_note: Nominally significant secondary P1NP increase at 2 weeks.

provenance.note: Posted secondary outcome group values and D+Q versus control p-value.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract P1NP secondary endpoint sentence
provenance.note: Abstract reports +16% relative to control and P=0.020 at 2 weeks.

record.statement: D+Q increased the secondary P1NP bone formation marker versus untreated control at 2 weeks.
caveats: Secondary endpoint; do not treat as overturning the null primary CTx result.
Registry provides full-range dispersion; abstract reports relative difference.

record.name: D+Q 4-week P1NP positive secondary result

effect.raw_text: Registry medians were D+Q 4.2 and control -12.1; PubMed abstract describes a +16% increase relative to control at 4 weeks.

analysis.effect_note: Nominally significant secondary P1NP increase at 4 weeks.

provenance.note: Posted secondary outcome group values and D+Q versus control p-value.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract P1NP secondary endpoint sentence
provenance.note: Abstract reports +16% relative to control and P=0.024 at 4 weeks.

record.statement: D+Q increased the secondary P1NP bone formation marker versus untreated control at 4 weeks.
caveats: Secondary endpoint; do not treat as overturning the null primary CTx result.
The registry full range for D+Q is wide, including an upper limit of 1480.

record.name: D+Q 2-week SASP null result

effect.raw_text: ClinicalTrials.gov posted median SASP percent change values for D+Q and untreated control; D+Q versus untreated control p=0.953.

analysis.effect_note: No statistically significant D+Q effect on posted 2-week SASP measure.

provenance.note: Posted SASP outcome group values and D+Q versus control p-value.

record.statement: D+Q did not significantly change the posted 2-week plasma SASP measure versus untreated control.
caveats: The registry outcome description is broad; marker-level details should be checked against the publication before mechanistic synthesis.

record.classification_basis: ClinicalTrials.gov registry record and API payload are treated as public registry data suitable for retained raw payloads, normalized section artifacts, source hashes, provenance locators, and structured extraction.

record.name: D+Q postmenopausal women bone metabolism phase 2 randomized trial
record.summary: Phase 2 randomized controlled trial of intermittent senolytic therapy in postmenopausal women. The D+Q versus untreated-control comparison reported in PubMed includes 60 participants, while the ClinicalTrials.gov posted-results record includes 74 participants across D+Q, fisetin, and untreated-control arms.

provenance.note: Abstract reports the D+Q versus control RCT comparison in postmenopausal women.
Registry provides posted outcome and adverse-event modules for the completed phase 2 trial.

record.name: D+Q bone RCT 20-week BMD synthesis compatibility
record.question: Can the 20-week BMD result from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.endpoint_family: DXA bone mineral density percent change by lumbar spine, femoral neck, and radius site.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Site-specific between-group BMD percent-change effect with uncertainty.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No pooling model selected because the registry record is descriptive, site-combined, and lacks pairwise site-specific effects with uncertainty.

record.pooling_rationale: Pooling is blocked by result count and descriptive-only extraction. The BMD record contains site-stratified group medians and full ranges, but no posted pairwise D+Q versus control analysis for an overall BMD endpoint and no site-specific effect values or uncertainty estimates. Lumbar spine, femoral neck, and radius values should not be collapsed into one pooled effect without site-specific outcome definitions.
record.non_pooling_reason: Do not pool BMD until site-specific outcomes or a pre-specified composite BMD metric are extracted with effect values, uncertainty, comparison, sample size, and group-value fields from at least two compatible results.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The result is descriptive-only: it has site-stratified group summaries but no pairwise overall or site-specific D+Q effect value with variance-compatible uncertainty.

self_healing_actions: Keep BMD pooling blocked until site-specific effect records are extracted or a defensible composite BMD outcome is defined.
Route BMD extraction to extraction_refresh if full text or supplements provide site-level pairwise estimates with uncertainty.

provenance.note: Posted BMD group values by lumbar spine, femoral neck, and radius classes.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract exploratory BMD subgroup sentence
provenance.note: Abstract mentions an exploratory radius BMD subgroup signal, which is not the same as the overall registry-posted BMD summary.

record.name: D+Q bone RCT 20-week CTx synthesis compatibility
record.question: Can the 20-week CTx result from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Between-group CTx percent-change effect with uncertainty.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No pooling model selected because only one result is available and the effect record is p-value-only.

record.pooling_rationale: Pooling is blocked by result count and extraction completeness. This endpoint-specific group has one CTx result from one trial, while the pooling requirement is at least two compatible results. The registry record contains arm medians, full ranges, and a Wilcoxon p-value, but no extractable between-group effect value or CI, standard error, or variance.
record.non_pooling_reason: Do not pool this primary CTx endpoint until at least one additional compatible CTx result is available and every included result has an effect value, uncertainty estimate, comparison, sample size, and required group-value fields.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The result is a p-value-only overall CTx comparison with group medians and full ranges; full range is not a variance estimate, and only one endpoint result is available.

self_healing_actions: Keep this endpoint group in pooling_blocked state until effect uncertainty and at least one additional compatible CTx result are available.
Route p-value-only CTx extraction to extraction_refresh if full text or supplements provide a between-group estimate with uncertainty.

provenance.note: Posted primary CTx outcome group values and D+Q versus untreated-control p-value.
Abstract confirms no significant overall CTx difference between groups.

record.name: D+Q bone RCT 20-week P1NP synthesis compatibility
record.question: Can the 20-week P1NP result from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Between-group P1NP percent-change effect with uncertainty.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No pooling model selected because only one 20-week P1NP result is available and the effect record is p-value-only.

record.pooling_rationale: Pooling is blocked by result count and missing effect extraction. This endpoint-specific group has one 20-week P1NP result from one trial, and the posted record provides arm medians, full ranges, and a p-value rather than a between-group effect value with uncertainty.
record.non_pooling_reason: Do not pool the 20-week P1NP endpoint until at least two compatible 20-week P1NP results are available and all included results carry effect value, uncertainty, comparison, sample-size, and group-value fields.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The 20-week P1NP result is p-value-only with group medians and full ranges; it lacks a between-group effect value and variance-compatible uncertainty.

self_healing_actions: Keep this endpoint group separate from short-term P1NP signals because the 20-week time horizon and direction differ.
Route p-value-only 20-week P1NP extraction to extraction_refresh if source tables provide an effect estimate with uncertainty.

provenance.note: Posted 20-week P1NP group values and D+Q versus untreated-control p-value.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract P1NP secondary endpoint sentence
provenance.note: Abstract reports P1NP was not different from untreated control at 20 weeks.

record.name: D+Q bone RCT short-term P1NP synthesis compatibility
record.question: Can the short-term 2-week and 4-week P1NP results from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.time_horizon: Baseline to 2 weeks and baseline to 4 weeks; not a single compatible time horizon.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Percentage-point difference relative to control with uncertainty.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No pooling model selected because the two short-term timepoints are repeated measures from one trial and lack uncertainty estimates.

record.pooling_rationale: Pooling is blocked despite two result records because the records are different time horizons from the same trial, not independent compatible studies. Both encode a +16 percentage-point short-term P1NP signal, but neither result has a CI, standard error, or variance. A formal synthesis would need either a pre-specified single time horizon or a longitudinal/repeated-measures model plus uncertainty.
record.non_pooling_reason: Do not compute a pooled short-term P1NP estimate across the 2-week and 4-week records unless the time horizon is made compatible and each result has an uncertainty estimate; treat these as related short-term signals in narrative synthesis.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The 2-week result has an effect value but no CI, standard error, or variance, and it should not be pooled directly with the 4-week timepoint as the same horizon.
The 4-week result has an effect value but no CI, standard error, or variance, and it should not be pooled directly with the 2-week timepoint as the same horizon.

self_healing_actions: Keep 2-week and 4-week P1NP as non-pooled related signals unless a longitudinal synthesis plan is added.
Route P1NP extraction to extraction_refresh if full text or supplements provide uncertainty estimates for either short-term timepoint.

provenance.note: Posted 2-week and 4-week P1NP group values and D+Q versus untreated-control p-values.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract P1NP secondary endpoint sentence
provenance.note: Abstract reports +16 percent relative to control at 2 and 4 weeks with nominal p-values.

record.name: D+Q bone RCT 20-week safety synthesis compatibility
record.question: Can the 20-week aggregate adverse-event result from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a safety meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.endpoint_family: Adverse events, serious adverse events, and deaths through approximately 20 weeks.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Event-specific risk ratio or risk difference with preferred-term counts.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No safety pooling model selected because the record is aggregate/descriptive and lacks preferred-term event-specific counts and comparative effect estimates.

record.pooling_rationale: Pooling is blocked by result count and safety event-specific extraction. This group has one aggregate safety result from one trial. It reports counts for other adverse events, serious adverse events, and deaths, but it does not enumerate preferred terms, event-specific denominators by preferred term, or a D+Q versus control risk ratio, risk difference, CI, standard error, or variance.
record.non_pooling_reason: Do not pool safety until adverse events are extracted as event-specific preferred terms with arm-level counts, denominators, a comparison, and effect uncertainty from at least two compatible safety results.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The result is an aggregate safety_event record, not preferred-term event-specific extraction; it lacks comparative effect value, uncertainty, and explicit event-specific preferred-term counts.

self_healing_actions: Keep safety pooling blocked until adverse events are split into event-specific preferred-term result records.
Route safety extraction to extraction_refresh if registry or publication tables can support event-specific arm counts and comparative safety effects.

provenance.note: Posted aggregate adverse-event counts for D+Q and untreated-control event groups.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract serious adverse event sentence
provenance.note: Abstract reports no serious adverse events were observed.

record.name: D+Q bone RCT 2-week SASP synthesis compatibility
record.question: Can the 2-week SASP result from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.endpoint_family: Plasma SASP marker burden percent change.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Marker-defined SASP percent-change effect with uncertainty.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No pooling model selected because only one broad SASP result is available and marker-level identities plus uncertainty are absent.

record.pooling_rationale: Pooling is blocked by result count, broad marker definition, and p-value-only extraction. This group has one SASP result from one trial. The registry record provides group medians, full ranges, and a p-value for a broad SASP burden measure, but not marker-level identities, an effect value, or a variance-compatible uncertainty estimate.
record.non_pooling_reason: Do not pool SASP effects until marker identity is harmonized and at least two compatible marker-level or composite SASP results provide effect value, uncertainty, comparison, sample size, and group-value fields.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The SASP result is a broad p-value-only measure without marker-level identity, effect value, or CI, standard error, or variance.

self_healing_actions: Keep SASP pooling blocked until marker-level identity and uncertainty fields are extracted.
Route SASP extraction to extraction_refresh if publication tables or supplements identify the marker panel and effect uncertainty.

provenance.note: Posted broad SASP outcome group values and D+Q versus untreated-control p-value.

access_policy.basis: ClinicalTrials.gov API records are public registry data and are treated as safe for raw artifact retention, normalization, and structured extraction.

artifacts.notes: Exact ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 JSON payload matching the source_snapshot content hash.
Agent-readable normalized markdown view of key protocol and results modules.
Structured section index and source-module mapping for the normalized registry markdown.

limitations: This is a normalized ClinicalTrials.gov registry record, not an article full text.
Markdown section text is derived from selected registry modules; raw JSON remains the audit source.
